notes: Feizi, granted Qin by King Xiao of Zhou
Son of Qin Ying
Son of Qin Hou
Killed by Western Rong, made Daifu by King Xuan
Defeated Western Rong with 7000 troops, Western Frontier Daifu
First enfeoffed as feudal lord, saved Zhou, granted lands west of Qi
Established history records, expanded territory to Qi
Moved to Pingyang, destroyed Dangshe
Usurped throne at age 5, assassinated
First human sacrifice (66 people), established counties
Moved to Yongcheng, first Fuxia (伏) festival
Built Mizhi altar, defeated Jin at Heyang
Liang Bo and Rui Bo came to court
Hegemon of Western Rong, expanded 12 states, 1000 li territory
Battles with Jin
Jin killed Duke Ling
Defeated by Jin-led coalition
Saved Chu from Wu with 500 chariots
Sent 500 chariots to save Chu from Wu
Confucius served in Lu
Confucius died in year 12
Attacked Dali, Yiqu, solar eclipse recorded
Nanzheng rebelled, Yiqu attacked
Surrounded by ministers, committed suicide
Son Xian Gong not enthroned
First decree for officials to wear swords
Conquered Nanzheng from Shu
Killed with mother by Shuzhang Gai
Abolished human sacrifice, moved to Yueyang, defeated Jin at Shimen (60,000 heads)
Shang Yang reforms, moved capital to Xianyang, 41 counties
First to take title King, Shang Yang executed
Died lifting bronze tripod, first Prime Ministers appointed
Longest reign, Bai Qi's victories, nine ding acquired
Shortest reign
Lü Buwei as chancellor, conquered Eastern Zhou
Unified China, 36 commanderies, First Emperor
Killed by Zhao Gao
Last Qin ruler, killed by rebels
